pairs.question: Where is Barnawartha station located, and what railway lines does it lie between?
pairs.answer: Barnawartha station is located in the town of Barnawartha on the North East railway in Victoria, Australia. It lies between the Melbourne–Albury standard gauge line, opened in 1962, and the former broad gauge line, which was converted to standard gauge in 2010.

pairs.question: What notable event occurred at Barnawartha station on 17 June 1982?
pairs.answer: On 17 June 1982, a collision occurred north of Barnawartha station between a Melbourne-bound goods train and the Melbourne-bound Spirit of Progress. The goods train ran past a red signal and collided with the stationary Spirit of Progress at 46 km/h, killing the two-man crew of the goods train and injuring nine others. The accident happened due to heavy fog and the lack of two-way radio systems on the locomotive.

pairs.question: What structural changes were made to Barnawartha station in 1987?
pairs.answer: In 1987, several alterations were made at Barnawartha station, including the removal of four signal posts, multiple points, the former platform road (No. 1 track), and the dead-end extension of No. 3 road.

pairs.question: How did the goods shed at Barnawartha station evolve over time?
pairs.answer: Barnawartha station opened with a temporary passenger shelter and a large brick goods shed in 1873. The brick goods shed was constructed in 1873 and remained in use until the station's closure in 1985, after which it became disused as sidings were removed.

pairs.question: What was the significance of the station building construction in 1889?
pairs.answer: In 1889, a permanent brick station building was erected at Barnawartha. This marked a transition from temporary infrastructure to a more permanent structure, reflecting the growing importance of the station at the time.

pairs.question: Why was the goods train unable to receive warnings about the collision risk in 1982?
pairs.answer: The goods train crew could not be warned about the danger because the locomotives lacked two-way radio systems at the time, which prevented train controllers in Melbourne from communicating the hazard of the red signal the train had passed.

pairs.question: What changes occurred to the track configuration at Barnawartha station over the years?
pairs.answer: Initially, tracks were extended in 1925 to accommodate longer trains. Later, in 1985, the west track was realigned away from the platform, and in 1987, further alterations included the removal of the platform road (No. 1 track) and the dead-end extension of No. 3 road.
